locations.raw_source_name: Alzheimer's & dementia : the journal of the Alzheimer's Association

crossref.abstract:
<jats:title>Abstract</jats:title><jats:sec><jats:title>INTRODUCTION</jats:title><jats:p>Blood‐based biomarkers offer a promising approach for the detection of neuropathologies from repetitive head impacts (RHI). We evaluated plasma biomarkers of amyloid, tau, neurodegeneration, and inflammation in former football players.</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>METHODS</jats:title><jats:p>The sample included 180 former football players and 60 asymptomatic, unexposed male participants (aged 45–74). Plasma assays were conducted for beta‐amyloid (Aβ) 40, Aβ42, hyper‐phosphorylated tau (p‐tau) 181+231, total tau (t‐tau), neurofilament light (NfL), glial fibrillary acidic protein (GFAP), interleukin‐6 (IL‐6), Aβ42/p‐tau181 and Aβ42/Aβ40 ratios. We evaluated their ability to differentiate the groups and associations with RHI proxies and traumatic encephalopathy syndrome (TES).</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>RESULTS</jats:title><jats:p>P‐tau181 and p‐tau231(p<jats:sub>adj </jats:sub>= 0.016) were higher and Aβ42/p‐tau181 was lower(p<jats:sub>adj </jats:sub>= 0.004) in football players compared to controls. Discrimination accuracy for p‐tau was modest (area under the curve [AUC] = 0.742). Effects were not attributable to AD‐related pathology. Younger age of first exposure (AFE) correlated with higher NfL (p<jats:sub>adj </jats:sub>= 0.03) and GFAP (p<jats:sub>adj </jats:sub>= 0.033). Plasma GFAP was higher in TES‐chronic traumatic encephalopathy (TES‐CTE) Possible/Probable (p<jats:sub>adj </jats:sub>= 0.008).</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>DISCUSSION</jats:title><jats:p>Plasma p‐tau181 and p‐tau231, GFAP, and NfL may offer some usefulness for the characterization of RHI‐related neuropathologies.</jats:p></jats:sec><jats:sec><jats:title>Highlights</jats:title><jats:p><jats:list list-type="bullet">
<jats:list-item><jats:p>Former football players had higher plasma p‐tau181 and p‐tau231 and lower Aβ42/ptau‐181 compared to asymptomatic, unexposed men.</jats:p></jats:list-item>
<jats:list-item><jats:p>Younger age of first exposure was associated with increased plasma NfL and GFAP in older but not younger participants.</jats:p></jats:list-item>
<jats:list-item><jats:p>Plasma GFAP was higher in participants with TES‐CTE possible/probable compared to TES‐CTE no/suggestive.</jats:p></jats:list-item>
</jats:list></jats:p></jats:sec>
title: Examination of plasma biomarkers of amyloid, tau, neurodegeneration, and neuroinflammation in former elite American football players
